authority_policy: Campaign openings are admitted only after the rebuild-and-diff gate proves source persistence.

nodes.label: Three-Way Selected-Skeleton Coherence
nodes.summary: CORRECTED 2026-07-16: global three-arm natural isomorphism at C_2 REFUTED by the wall-isotropy/stabilizer obstruction; forks into C2a chamber coherence (open) and C2b wall-isotropy comparison theorem (proved). Lorentzian gauge-selection branch refuted by divisibility of ker(Sigma). Remaining: two typed functors on the regular chamber + precision two-cover resolution.

retrieval_questions: Why is the global three-arm isomorphism impossible?
Which arms have fixed-point vs free-orbit walls?
Why does IEEE-754 need a tie convention when GR does not?

edges.label: DBP:campaign:tssc depends_on DBP:thm:r3_acfold
edges.basis: AC-fold R3 supplies the typed selected skeleton; its wall is fixed-point type per the isotropy theorem.

edges.label: DBP:campaign:tssc depends_on DBP:wall:V_faithful
edges.basis: Kernel-2Z non-faithfulness is the common C_2 floor for the chamber-level comparison; walls excluded by the isotropy obstruction.

edges.label: DBP:campaign:tssc depends_on PFT:draft:rf_pred006
edges.basis: P4's free-orbit obstruction is located in the precision output bundle; the wall-isotropy classification depends on PRED-006's two-cover split.

edges.label: DBP:campaign:tssc opens_gap U-1918
edges.basis: U-1918 is the headline open claim: two DBP-arm functors into the shared skeleton plus componentwise coherence diagrams.

edges.label: DBP:campaign:tssc proves U-1918
edges.basis: The wall-isotropy theorem is the proved fork C2b of the campaign's headline claim; source doc WALL_ISOTROPY_COMPARISON_THEOREM_2026-07-16.md, sha256 8d080e07fc1feda079fcd0bcd9f01adfb1596368e690d4874b89163b24fdfae7.

edges.label: U-1918 depends_on DBP:wall:V_faithful
edges.basis: Coherence is stated at C_2 after mod-2 reduction; the kernel-2Z non-faithfulness is the common floor rather than an obstruction.
